name: CrackMapExec / NetExec (Network Penetration Testing)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2-Clause); Pennyw0rth (NetExec fork of CrackMapExec)

performance.notes: Network-dependent; parallel threads for credential spraying

agent_readiness.notes: Swiss Army knife for Windows/Active Directory penetration testing. netexec smb 192.168.1.0/24 -u user -p pass. Protocols: SMB, WinRM, LDAP, SSH, RDP, MSSQL, FTP. --shares (enumerate shares), --sam (dump SAM), --lsa (dump LSA secrets), -x 'cmd' (execute commands), --spider (file spider). Pass-the-hash: -H NTLM_HASH. BloodHound collection: --bloodhound. Credential spraying across subnet. Successor to CrackMapExec (CME). Required for OSCP/CRTP/CRTE AD labs.

gotchas: Only use on authorized targets — extremely invasive
Account lockout risk during credential spraying
Detected by EDR/SIEM in most enterprise environments
NetExec (nxc) is the maintained fork; CrackMapExec (cme) is deprecated
best_when: Internal network assessments (credential spraying, lateral movement)
avoid_when: Production environments without explicit written authorization
External testing (designed for internal AD environments)
summary: NetExec (successor to CrackMapExec) is the standard tool for Windows/AD penetration testing. Tests SMB/WinRM/LDAP/SSH/RDP across subnets. Credential spraying, pass-the-hash, SAM/LSA dumping, share enumeration, command execution, BloodHound collection. Essential for OSCP/CRTP labs. nxc command (NetExec) is the maintained fork. Only for authorized internal network assessments.
